items.title: The Legend of the Red Spider Lily
items.lead: The flower that blooms along the path of souls.

cardImage.alt: Red Spider Lily tattoo with black ink on an arm.

heroImage.alt: Moonlit Japanese path filled with Red Spider Lilies.

sections.title: A flower that should never be taken home
paragraphs: Deep within Japanese folklore grows a flower unlike any other. Known as the Red Spider Lily, or Higanbana, it appears just as summer fades and autumn begins.
Its crimson petals resemble flames dancing in the wind, carrying a mysterious reputation for centuries.

According to Japanese tradition, these flowers bloom along the paths that guide departed souls toward the afterlife. For this reason, they are often found surrounding ancient cemeteries and temples, silently marking the boundary between the living and the dead.

sections.title: Two spirits forbidden to meet
paragraphs: An old legend tells of two spirits. One protected the flower. The other protected its leaves.
They were forbidden to meet. Whenever the flowers bloomed, the leaves disappeared. When the leaves returned, the flowers had already fallen.

Condemned to miss each other forever, the story became a symbol of impossible love, separation and eternal longing. Even today, many Japanese associate the Red Spider Lily with memories that never truly fade.

sections.title: What it represents
paragraphs: Higanbana does not speak of a simple ending. It speaks of passage, memory and bonds that remain even when physical presence disappears.

sections.title: Beauty within loss
paragraphs: At Akai Hana Design Studio, we see the Red Spider Lily as more than a flower. It represents memory, transformation and beauty even within loss.
Its flowing petals make it a strong source of inspiration for tattoos, illustration, branding, concept art and editorial pieces with a delicate but intense presence.

gallery.alt: Red Spider Lily illustration on a dark background.
Torii path with a red umbrella figure among Higanbana flowers.
Circular Higanbana composition inspired by yin and yang.
Kitsune mask inspired by red flowers.
Dark oni surrounded by red Higanbana flowers.
Geisha in a moonlit composition with Higanbana flowers.
Torii shrine among red flowers and a nocturnal atmosphere.

items.quote: Every design tells a story. Some stories never stop blooming.

items.title: Kitsune
items.lead: A fox spirit tied to intuition, transformation and mystery.

cardImage.alt: White Kitsune tattoo with red moon, tails and torii.

heroImage.alt: White nine-tailed Kitsune under the moon and cherry blossoms.

sections.title: The spirit of the nine tails
paragraphs: In ancient Japanese legends, Kitsune are fox spirits able to live for hundreds of years, gain wisdom and power, and even change their form.
The more tails a Kitsune has, the older and more powerful it is. The nine-tailed Kitsune is considered almost divine, a being of knowledge, magic and intuition.

sections.title: Guardians, messengers and trials
paragraphs: They can be guardians, messengers or protectors of humanity, especially as servants of Inari, deity of prosperity and abundance.
Some Kitsune test humans through illusions and challenges, teaching humility and wisdom to those who encounter them.

sections.title: Associated symbols
paragraphs: The Kitsune lives between the visible and the hidden. Its beauty is not only ornamental: it represents intelligence, change, spiritual connection and protection.

sections.title: A story of adaptation and bond
paragraphs: To tattoo a Kitsune is to carry a symbol of personal transformation, intelligence and spiritual connection on the skin.
It represents the ability to adapt, overcome obstacles and protect what you value most. Every tail tells a story. Every gaze, a secret. Every tattoo, an eternal bond with the legend.

items.quote: A Kitsune does not reveal its true form until it finds someone worthy of knowing the truth.

items.title: Koi
items.romanized: The carp that defied the current
items.lead: A legend of perseverance, transformation and destiny.

cardImage.alt: Red Koi tattoo among waves and sakura flowers.

heroImage.alt: Koi climbing a waterfall toward a torii beneath the red moon.

sections.title: The carp that moves against the current
paragraphs: The Koi is a carp from Japan, admired for its beauty, strength and ability to swim even against difficult currents.
In Japanese imagery it is more than an ornamental fish. It represents a life built through patience, resistance and determination.

sections.title: The Dragon Gate
paragraphs: An old legend tells of thousands of carp trying to swim against a powerful current to reach the top of a waterfall.
Most gave up, defeated by the force of the water and exhaustion. But one carp continued, year after year, overcoming each fall and every storm.
When it reached the top, the gods recognized its courage and transformed it into a dragon. Since then, the Koi has represented perseverance turning a dream into destiny.

sections.title: What it symbolizes
paragraphs: The Koi speaks of moving forward even when the path is heavy. Its energy combines movement, balance and personal transformation.

sections.title: A symbol for continuing forward
paragraphs: To tattoo a Koi is to carry a story of struggle, growth and change on the skin. It is a reminder that every obstacle can also be a current pushing us toward a stronger version of ourselves.
It can be designed alone to symbolize the personal journey, or paired with water, lotus flowers, sakura or dragons to deepen its spiritual meaning.

gallery.alt: Koi rising through foam and current.
Koi before the waterfall and the silhouette of a dragon.

items.quote: Keep swimming. The waterfall is part of the path too.

items.title: Ryū
items.lead: A guardian of water, sky and spiritual wisdom.

cardImage.alt: Japanese Ryū dragon tattoo with red eyes and lightning.

heroImage.alt: Japanese Ryū dragon among clouds, sakura and a golden moon.

sections.title: The dragon that protects balance
paragraphs: Ryū is not a creature of destruction. In Japanese tradition it is tied to water, sky, rain and deep wisdom.
Unlike many dragons in Western imagery, the Japanese dragon often appears as a benevolent protector: an ancient presence connecting earth, clouds and seas.

sections.title: The guardian who brought the rain
paragraphs: One legend tells that during a great drought, rivers began to vanish and the fields fell silent. People prayed to the heavens, but the clouds did not answer.
Then a Ryū emerged from the depths of the water and rose through the mist. As its serpentine body crossed the sky, it opened the clouds and returned rain to the land.
Since then, the dragon has been honored as a guardian of life, protection and abundance.

sections.title: What it represents
paragraphs: Ryū symbolizes immense strength that does not need to destroy to leave a mark. Its power comes from control, calm and spiritual connection.

sections.title: Strength that flows without breaking
paragraphs: To tattoo a Ryū is to carry a symbol of protection, direction and spiritual strength. It represents someone who crosses storms without losing their center.
Its serpentine body allows powerful and elegant compositions: it can wrap around the arm, back or torso, paired with clouds, water, sakura or inner fire.

gallery.alt: Detail of the face and body of a Japanese Ryū dragon.
Japanese landscape with mountains, red moon and temple beneath the dragon.

items.quote: True strength does not always roar. Sometimes it guides the current.

values.title: Custom Tattoo Design

intro.title: Custom design with a Japanese soul.
intro.body: We are not a shop and we do not manufacture products. We create bespoke digital designs for tattoos, illustration, branding, logos, visual identity, Japanese art and creative projects that need a memorable presence.

customDesigns.title: Custom design services
customDesigns.body: Digital concepts for tattoos, Japanese illustration and creative projects that need a memorable visual presence.
customDesigns.note: Akai Hana does not do physical tattoos. We create custom artwork that can be used as inspiration, reference or taken to the artist of your choice.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.body: A simple process to turn an idea into a carefully crafted digital piece.

steps.title: You tell me your idea
steps.body: You share the story, symbols, references, aesthetic or emotion you want to turn into a design.

steps.title: We define style, format and use
steps.body: We shape the medium, format, detail level, color direction, references and technical boundaries.

steps.title: I create one or more proposals
steps.body: I prepare digital proposals with composition, atmosphere and a coherent visual direction.

steps.title: We refine the details
steps.body: We polish symbols, proportions, intensity, contrast and the overall reading of the design.

steps.title: You receive the final design
steps.body: I deliver the final high-resolution file so you can use it as reference, visual art or production base.

faq.title: Before requesting your design
faq.body: Clear answers about what the service includes and how to use a custom digital design.

items.question: Do you do physical tattoos?
items.answer: No. Akai Hana Design Studio creates custom digital designs and concepts. If you want a piece tattooed, the tattoo itself must be applied by a professional tattoo artist in an authorized studio.

items.question: What do I receive when I commission a design?
items.answer: You receive a high-resolution digital file with a visual proposal prepared to review, share and take to your tattoo artist.

items.question: Can I request changes?
items.answer: Yes. The process includes adjustments to refine composition, symbols, contrast, style and details before closing the final file.

items.question: Can I take the design to my tattoo artist?
items.answer: Yes. That is the purpose of the service: to give you a clear visual base to discuss with your tattoo artist and adapt technically if needed.

items.question: Do you create designs in color and black and white?
items.answer: Yes. I can work in black and white, color, black with crimson details or a mixed visual direction depending on your idea.

items.question: How long does a custom design take?
items.answer: It depends on size and complexity. After reviewing your request I will confirm scope, estimated timing and next steps.

discord.title: Talk to me on Discord
discord.body: Prefer to tell me your idea before sending the form? Add me on Discord and we can talk about references, size, body placement, style and availability.
discord.label: Discord username

about.title: Premium art with Japanese roots.
about.bodyOne: Akai Hana Design Studio turns personal ideas into digital pieces with atmosphere, symbol and intention.
about.bodyTwo: Anime, dark fantasy, Japanese landscape and illustration come together in a restrained, exclusive visual direction.
about.statementOne: Akai Hana Design Studio was born from a deep passion for art, symbols and Japanese culture. Every creation is developed with attention to detail, seeking harmony between aesthetics, meaning and emotion.
about.statementTwo: We work so each piece does not only look beautiful, but also tells something that represents you.
